# services.tt-rss

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.tt-rss`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.tt-rss.auth.autoCreate` | `boolean` | Allow authentication modules to auto-create users in tt-rss internal database when authenticated successfully. |
| `services.tt-rss.auth.autoLogin` | `boolean` | Automatically login user on remote or other kind of externally supplied authentication, otherwise redirect to login form as normal. If set to true, users won't be able to set application language and settings profile. |
| `services.tt-rss.database.createLocally` | `boolean` | Create the database and database user locally. |
| `services.tt-rss.database.host` | `null or string` | Host of the database. Leave null to use Unix domain socket. |
| `services.tt-rss.database.name` | `string` | Name of the existing database. |
| `services.tt-rss.database.password` | `null or string` | The database user's password. |
| `services.tt-rss.database.passwordFile` | `null or string` | The database user's password. |
| `services.tt-rss.database.port` | `null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The database's port. If not set, the default ports will be provided (5432 and 3306 for pgsql and mysql respectively). |
| `services.tt-rss.database.type` | `one of "pgsql", "mysql"` | Database to store feeds. Supported are pgsql and mysql. |
| `services.tt-rss.database.user` | `string` | The database user. The user must exist and has access to the specified database. |
| `services.tt-rss.email.digestSubject` | `string` | Subject line for email digests. |
| `services.tt-rss.email.fromAddress` | `string` | Address for sending outgoing mail. This applies to password reset notifications, digest emails and any other mail. |
| `services.tt-rss.email.fromName` | `string` | Name for sending outgoing mail. This applies to password reset notifications, digest emails and any other mail. |
| `services.tt-rss.email.login` | `string` | SMTP authentication login used when sending outgoing mail. |
| `services.tt-rss.email.password` | `string` | SMTP authentication password used when sending outgoing mail. |
| `services.tt-rss.email.security` | `one of "", "ssl", "tls"` | Used to select a secure SMTP connection. Allowed values: ssl, tls, or empty. |
| `services.tt-rss.email.server` | `string` | Hostname:port combination to send outgoing mail. Blank - use system MTA. |
| `services.tt-rss.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable tt-rss. |
| `services.tt-rss.enableGZipOutput` | `boolean` | Selectively gzip output to improve wire performance. This requires PHP Zlib extension on the server. Enabling this can break tt-rss in several httpd/php configurations, if you experience weird errors and tt-rss failing to start, blank pages after login, or content encoding errors, disable it. |
| `services.tt-rss.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Additional lines to append to `config.php`. |
| `services.tt-rss.feedCryptKey` | `string` | Key used for encryption of passwords for password-protected feeds in the database. A string of 24 random characters. If left blank, encryption is not used. Requires mcrypt functions. Warning: changing this key will make your stored feed passwords impossible to decrypt. |
| `services.tt-rss.forceArticlePurge` | `signed integer` | When this option is not 0, users ability to control feed purging intervals is disabled and all articles (which are not starred) older than this amount of days are purged. |
| `services.tt-rss.logDestination` | `one of "", "sql", "syslog"` | Log destination to use. Possible values: sql (uses internal logging you can read in Preferences -> System), syslog - logs to system log. Setting this to blank uses PHP logging (usually to http server error.log). |
| `services.tt-rss.phpPackage` | `package` | php package to use for php fpm and update daemon. |
| `services.tt-rss.pluginPackages` | `list of package` | List of plugins to install. The list elements are expected to be derivations. All elements in this derivation are automatically copied to the `plugins.local` directory. |
| `services.tt-rss.plugins` | `list of string` | List of plugins to load automatically for all users. System plugins have to be specified here. Please enable at least one authentication plugin here (auth\_\*). Users may enable other user plugins from Preferences/Plugins but may not disable plugins specified in this list. Disabling auth_internal in this list would automatically disable reset password link on the login form. |
| `services.tt-rss.pool` | `string` | Name of existing phpfpm pool that is used to run web-application. If not specified a pool will be created automatically with default values. |
| `services.tt-rss.pubSubHubbub.enable` | `boolean` | Enable client PubSubHubbub support in tt-rss. When disabled, tt-rss won't try to subscribe to PUSH feed updates. |
| `services.tt-rss.pubSubHubbub.hub` | `string` | URL to a PubSubHubbub-compatible hub server. If defined, "Published articles" generated feed would automatically become PUSH-enabled. |
| `services.tt-rss.registration.enable` | `boolean` | Allow users to register themselves. Please be aware that allowing random people to access your tt-rss installation is a security risk and potentially might lead to data loss or server exploit. Disabled by default. |
| `services.tt-rss.registration.maxUsers` | `signed integer` | Maximum amount of users which will be allowed to register on this system. 0 - no limit. |
| `services.tt-rss.registration.notifyAddress` | `string` | Email address to send new user notifications to. |
| `services.tt-rss.root` | `absolute path` | Root of the application. |
| `services.tt-rss.selfUrlPath` | `string` | Full URL of your tt-rss installation. This should be set to the location of tt-rss directory, e.g. http://example.org/tt-rss/ You need to set this option correctly otherwise several features including PUSH, bookmarklets and browser integration will not work properly. |
| `services.tt-rss.sessionCookieLifetime` | `signed integer` | Default lifetime of a session (e.g. login) cookie. In seconds, 0 means cookie will be deleted when browser closes. |
| `services.tt-rss.simpleUpdateMode` | `boolean` | Enables fallback update mode where tt-rss tries to update feeds in background while tt-rss is open in your browser. If you don't have a lot of feeds and don't want to or can't run background processes while not running tt-rss, this method is generally viable to keep your feeds up to date. Still, there are more robust (and recommended) updating methods available, you can read about them here: <https://tt-rss.org/wiki/UpdatingFeeds> |
| `services.tt-rss.singleUserMode` | `boolean` | Operate in single user mode, disables all functionality related to multiple users and authentication. Enabling this assumes you have your tt-rss directory protected by other means (e.g. http auth). |
| `services.tt-rss.sphinx.index` | `list of string` | Index names in Sphinx configuration. Example configuration files are available on tt-rss wiki. |
| `services.tt-rss.sphinx.server` | `string` | Hostname:port combination for the Sphinx server. |
| `services.tt-rss.themePackages` | `list of package` | List of themes to install. The list elements are expected to be derivations. All elements in this derivation are automatically copied to the `themes.local` directory. |
| `services.tt-rss.updateDaemon.commandFlags` | `string` | Command-line flags passed to the update daemon. The default --quiet flag mutes all logging, including errors. |
| `services.tt-rss.user` | `string` | User account under which both the update daemon and the web-application run. |
| `services.tt-rss.virtualHost` | `null or string` | Name of the nginx virtualhost to use and setup. If null, do not setup any virtualhost. |
